title: S4f: migrate hook-runtime (81 anchors, 8 unparsed) — WITH a duplicate rule id in source
action: Standard area migration — same STEP 0-4 contract as cell f2-5; freshest worked example is commit ab8cf6e (onboarding, 58 anchors, 8 concepts). TARGET docs/specs/hook-runtime.md, expect 81 anchors / 8 unparsed at 762 source lines. SPECIAL HAZARD, resolve it FIRST: the source contains a DUPLICATE rule id — R14 appears at roughly line 450 and again at roughly line 477 (verified by the f2-4 sweep and the f2-4 judge). The gate keys anchors by id in a Map, so the FIRST R14's text is overwritten by the second and becomes unmeasurable by the fidelity floor; a set-equality check cannot see the second member of a duplicate pair. Do NOT migrate until this is resolved. Resolve by DISAMBIGUATING IN THE SOURCE COPY AND THE PIN, not by dropping either rule: read both R14 blocks, confirm they are genuinely two distinct rules (if they are the same rule stated twice, say so and treat it as one), and if distinct, renumber the SECOND occurrence in docs/specs/hook-runtime.md to the next free id (e.g. R14a) BEFORE STEP 0 captures the pin — so the pinned blob already carries distinct ids and every anchor is individually measurable. Record the renumbering in the trace and in the stub's anchor map so a reader of the old id finds it. If renumbering is wrong for any reason, return [BLOCKED] with your reasoning rather than migrating a duplicate. Then proceed with the normal loop. Chain grows to 73 suites.

truths: the duplicate R14 is resolved BEFORE the pin is captured — both blocks quoted in the trace with the resolution and its reasoning
coverage: all anchors owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost — and the anchor count reflects the disambiguation
fidelity min/median quoted above the 0.60 floor with no concept edited; BOTH former-R14 rules individually measured
pin declares unparsed_blocks honestly; blob_sha verified against the committed copy
prohibitions: never migrate while two anchors share an id — that hides one from the fidelity floor permanently
never drop or merge a rule to remove the duplicate
never invent ids for unnumbered blocks (D10)
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the floor

trace.outcome: hook-runtime migrated — 12 concepts, 81-anchor stub map, duplicate R14 disambiguated to R14/R14a before the pin; gate in chain (73 suites)

  "block_A_L450_kept_R14": "- R14 \u2014 The session-stop handler emits a block verdict ONLY for the gate-bypass net (B15): only on the session-stop event, only in an approval-gate phase whose gate the active bypass level covers and is still pending, and at most once per (session, phase, gate, level) \u2014 an immediate same-key re-stop degrades to the ordinary advisory. It never fires on compaction, child-stop, a missing event, the exploring/Gate-1 phase, or a `normal`-lane hard-gate change (GitHub #18).",
  "block_B_L477_renumbered_R14a": "- R14a \u2014 The write guard's command-shape recognition accepts both the unified dispatcher form (group + verb) and the retired per-command helper form. The retired form is a transition affordance for hosts whose vendored tools predate the unified surface \u2014 it is slated for removal once hosts have upgraded (a debt item tracks it), and its recognition never revives the deleted scripts themselves (decision bbc6bcea, D3).",

Renumbering block B therefore churns ZERO external references and changes no citation's meaning; renumbering block A would have silently re-pointed all five.",

 "red_chain_found_and_fixed": "the first full-chain run went RED on two suites \u2014 'BUNDLE UNHEALTHY: OKF error unparseable_frontmatter in areas/advisor-protocol/zz-f2-2-unhealthy-fixture.md' plus three stale indexes \u2014 on okf_migrate --check hook-runtime and --check onboarding. Root cause is NOT this migration: test_okf_pins.mjs section 22 deliberately writes a non-canonical concept into the LIVE docs/knowledge/ bundle to prove the invariants are wired, and run_verify runs suites at concurrency 5, so any live-bundle reader scheduled inside that window fails for an unrelated reason. Pre-existing and latent; adding a 9th concurrent reader made it ~50/50 (the identical tree passed on the immediate re-run). Fixed by scheduling, not by weakening the assertion: run_verify now runs the mutator and every live-bundle reader (okf_migrate *, test_okf_pins, bee knowledge check|index --check) as ONE sequential unit that still runs concurrently with the ~60 bundle-free suites. Verified with 3 consecutive green chain runs plus the recorded one; wall clock unchanged (58s -> 60s).",

The only live citation outside docs/history/** is 'hook-runtime B15/R14' in skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md:213 and its .codex-plugin mirror; both anchors resolve through the stub's anchor map to advisories-and-turn-control.md, and R14 deliberately kept its id, so f2-3's precedent applies \u2014 no release machinery churned.",
 "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (the prior behavior this change alters), three measured reds:\n(1) THE HAZARD ITSELF \u2014 `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --inventory docs/specs/hook-runtime.md` at ab8cf6e derived rules ['R1','R2','R3','R4','R5','R6','R7','R8','R8a','R8b','R9','R10','R14','R11','R12','R13','R14','R15',...]: 81 anchor members but only 80 DISTINCT ids. Because inventorySpec keys anchor texts in a Map, the first R14's bytes were overwritten by the second's, so fidelityReport could only ever measure one of the two rules and set-equality could not see the pair's second member at all \u2014 a coverage gate that would have reported 81/81 PASS while one shipped rule was structurally unmeasurable. That is the state this cell had to end, and it could not be ended by migrating.\n(2) RED-FIRST on the new gate entry \u2014 with the R14a claim removed from write-guard-request-shapes.md's bee.sources: `FAIL okf_migrate --check hook-runtime: 81 anchors, 80 owned, 0 duplicated, 1 lost` / `- LOST in concepts: R14a is claimed by no concept's bee.sources (expected one owner)`. Restored to green. Proves the new chain suite actually fails when coverage is lost, rather than passing by construction.\n(3) RED on the full chain, before the scheduling fix \u2014 `FAIL run_verify: 73 suite(s)` with `FAIL okf_migrate --check hook-runtime (exit 1)` and `FAIL okf_migrate --check onboarding (exit 1)`, both naming `BUNDLE UNHEALTHY: OKF error unparseable_frontmatter in areas/advisor-protocol/zz-f2-2-unhealthy-fixture.md` plus `the generated indexes are stale (D21) ... 3 stale`. The identical tree then passed on the next run, which is what identified it as a live-bundle test race rather than a content failure.",
 "deliberate_exceptions": "The pin registry gained a new SHAPE (repaired_from/repair_reason), which is a brand-new surface with no prior behavior to characterize: before this cell, a blob_sha disagreeing with commit:path had exactly one meaning (PIN_SHA_MISMATCH, exit 1) and there was no way to express a source that had to be repaired before it could be pinned. That single pre-existing meaning is preserved unchanged for every undeclared, misdeclared, or unexplained disagreement, and the new branch is inert on all eight pre-existing pins \u2014 asserted from both sides in test_okf_pins.mjs section 15 (296 assertions green)."
}
